description: Trigger discrimination for GENERATING API reference (this skill) vs designing the API CONTRACT (api-event-architect), the general docs pipeline (docs-as-code-architect), and organizing the corpus by mode (diataxis-doc-organizer).

cases.prompt: Generate our API reference from the schema so it stays in sync, with validated examples and a version selector.
cases.note: SEAM CASE (primary) — generating reference from the source of truth to prevent drift is API-doc generation. No contract is being designed and no general pipeline built.
cases.reason: Designing generated, drift-proof API reference is this skill's core scope; it documents a contract designed elsewhere.

cases.prompt: Design the API contract: routes, response envelope, versioning and deprecation policy, idempotency, rate limits, and webhooks.
cases.note: SEAM CASE (primary, reverse — cross-pack) — routes, envelopes, versioning policy, and rate limits are the contract itself, not its reference docs.
cases.reason: api-event-architect defines the API contract. This skill generates the reference that documents it.

cases.prompt: Set up the whole docs build: generator, PR previews, CI checks, and versioned deploys for all our documentation.
cases.reason: docs-as-code-architect designs the pipeline for all docs. API-reference generation is one slice that plugs into it.

cases.prompt: Organize all our docs into tutorials, how-to, reference, and explanation.
cases.reason: diataxis-doc-organizer classifies the whole corpus by mode. This skill generates the reference mode for an API; it doesn't organize the set.
